changesProposed.name: Icarus

current.name: Icarus
current.founder: Henry Kwan (CEO, fmr Orbital — built spacecraft + space robots, helped win $935M NASA space habitat contract; fmr NASA flight sciences drone designer; M.S. Aerospace Engineering Georgia Tech; TS/SCI clearance)

verified.name: Icarus
verified.description: Designs and manufactures solar-powered, autonomous stratospheric aircraft operating above 60,000 feet for weeks-long missions. The flagship platform APOLLO is a fully solar-powered, autonomous aircraft that first flew in 2024.
verified.notes: Source [2] is Wikipedia article about Greek mythology (not relevant). Sources [3] and [4] reference a different company 'Icarus Robotics' (space robots for ISS), not the stratospheric aircraft company. Only sources [0] and [1] are relevant to the aerospace company. Y Combinator confirmed as investor from company website; Paul Graham listed as backer on About page. Seed stage inferred from database entry but not explicitly stated in provided sources. Total raised and valuation not disclosed in sources.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company that operates three divisions: Space (orbital launches), Connectivity (Starlink satellite communications), and Artificial intelligence (Grok and data centers).
verified.notes: Wikipedia source indicates IPO occurred on June 12, 2026, which raised $86 billion and was the largest IPO in history. Elon Musk owns 42% of outstanding shares and controls 85% voting power via super-voting stock (Wikipedia states 82% in one place and 85% in another). SpaceX acquired xAI in 2026 per Wikipedia source.

changesProposed.name: Terran Robotics

verified.name: Terran Robotics
verified.description: Company building terraforming robots that use AI and robotics to construct adobe homes from earth materials at reduced cost compared to traditional construction methods.
verified.notes: Founded in 2018 after receiving $256K NSF SBIR grant. Company operates lab in Bloomington, Indiana and Proto-Town near Austin, Texas. Source [2] is about Monumental (different company), not Terran Robotics, so excluded from verification.

changesProposed.name: Valar Atomics

verified.name: Valar Atomics
verified.description: Nuclear energy company building high-temperature gas reactors (HTGR) on gigasites to produce hydrogen, data center power, industrial power, and clean hydrocarbon fuels.
verified.notes: Database entry claimed $130M Series A and $149M total raised, but sources do not verify these figures. Source [2] indicates $6B valuation in funding talks as of July 2026. Location listed as 'El Segundo, CA' in database entry but not verified in sources; Ward-250 reactor is in Utah per source [0]. Database claim about 'first startup in history to split the atom' and 'zero-power criticality at Los Alamos' not found in sources.
